member.summary: Gets the and of the lock current held on the specified

The current lock type, one of the values from

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Client.JoobContext.SetImplicitUpdatingLockType(JadeSoftware.Joob.Client.LockType)
member.summary: Sets the implicit lock type that will be used when acquiring a lock against an object on behalf of the user, when the object is updated.
member.param: The updating lock type (must be either LockType.Exclusive, LockType.Update)

members.member:
Specifies a delegate method to be invoked when a JomLockException is raised. This method can retry the lock and return 'Continue' if successful. It can also return 'PassBack' to pass the exception back to other handlers, or 'Abort' to cause the lock exception to be thrown.
The delegate lock exception handler method to be added.

member.returns: A collection of objects that represent all of the instances of this type in the JOOB Database.

member.summary: Stores the reference information required to recreate a JoobObject. JoobObjects require a valid JoobContext in order to be used. member.summary: Extension class that adds functionality to a JoobObject for serialization/de-serialization

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Serialization.JoobObjectExtensions.Include``1(``0,System.String)
member.summary: When serializing, by default object references are not included in the result. To include object references specify the include path.
member.remarks:
Use ALLMEMBERS to include all the members of an instance. Note the object references of these members still need to be included separately
Company company = new Company(); company.Include("AllClients.ALLMEMBERS"); //will now include the company ands its clients. For each client include all object references. company.Include("AllBuilidngs").Include("AllEmployees"); //will now include the above plus the company buildings, and company employees

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.Class.Transient
member.summary: The read-only Transient property of the Class class specifies whether instances of the class are transient by default. This default value can be overridden when an object is created. Transient objects exist only for some predetermined time within an application session. They are not stored in the database and are destroyed when explicitly deleted or when the application terminates.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.Class.Instances
member.summary: The read-only Instances property of the Class class contains a reference to instances of the class that it represents. This property enables you to access class instances as though they were in a class collection without having to populate a collection.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.Class.ClassMapReferences
member.summary: The read-only ClassMapReferences property of the Class class is a collection of all of the class.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.Class.InstancesOfType``1
member.summary: The read-only InstancesOfType method of the Class class contains a reference to instances of the class that it represents. This property enables you to access class instances as though they were in a class collection without having to populate a collection.
member.remarks: This method InstancesOfType is appropriate when dealing with JOOB meta database style programming. The executable does not need the assemblies that contain the generated .Net classes from the JOML definitions.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.Class.AllInstances``1(System.Int64,System.Boolean)
member.summary: The InstancesOfType method of the Class class adds all persistent instances of the receiver class to the array specified in the objArray parameter.
member.param: The maxInstances parameter specifies the maximum number of instances returned in the ObjectArray parameter
If the includeSubclasses parameter is set to true, all sub-classes of the receiver class are also included in the array.
member.returns: ObjectArray of all instances of the receiver class and optionally all sub-classes

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.Class.AllProperties
member.summary: The AllProperties method of the Class class returns a reference to all properties of the class of the receiver.
member.returns: An array of Property objects

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.Class.AllPropertiesUpTo(JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.Class)
member.summary: The AllPropertiesUpTo method of the Class class returns a reference to all properties of super classes of the receiver in the current schema up to those of the class specified in the parameter.
member.returns: An array of Property objects
member.remarks: Only references to super class properties in the current schema are returned, and not those in super schemas.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.Class.CountPersistentInstances
member.summary:
The CountPersistentInstances method of the Class class returns the number of non-exclusive persistent instances of the receiver class.
The CountPersistentInstances method is significantly faster than the method on an instances pseudo-collection, as shown in the following code fragments.
context.AllInstances<Customer>().LongCount; Customer.CountPersistentInstances(); // significantly faster
However, this method impacts other users of the database file where the instances of the receiver class are stored. The best time to run this method is therefore when there are few other active users.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.Class.DeleteAllInstances
member.summary: Delete all user instances for this class.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.Class.CountPersistentInstancesLimit(System.Int32)
member.summary:
The CountPersistentInstancesLimit method of the Class class returns the number of non-exclusive persistent instances of the receiver class up to the limit specified.
The CountPersistentInstancesLimit method is significantly faster than the method on an instances pseudo-collection, as shown in the following code fragments. This method may be used instead of CountPersistentInstances to limit the time used, but the result will not be correct if actual count is greater than limit.
context.AllInstances<Customer>().LongCount; Customer.CountPersistentInstancesLimit(maxValue); // significantly faster
However, this method impacts other users of the database file where the instances of the receiver class are stored. The best time to run this method is therefore when there are few other active users.
member.param: The limit on the number of instances to return. If the value returned is greater than this value, the actual count is some number greater than limit.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.Path

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.BackupFile(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: The BackupFile method of the DatabaseFile class initiates a back up of the physical database file to the directory specified in the backupDirectory parameter. (The backup directory must be a valid directory on the database server.) This method executes on the database server node, and is implemented and executed by the database engine. The backup session performs various consistency checks similar to a database certify, to ensure the integrity of the backup.
member.param: Directory to back database file up to

Set the verifyChecksums parameter to true if you want checksums verified in the backed up file. Checksum verification is performed in a separate pass of the backed up file immediately after the copy phase. A checksum analysis of your backed up database files verifies that the files have not been corrupted by a hardware or environmental problem during the backup session. You should perform a separate checksum analysis of any backup that has been moved across media, especially if transferred across a network.
Set the compress parameter to true if you want to compress backed up data. You can compress data in a checked or an unchecked backup.

Set the overwriteDestination parameter to true if you want to allow file backups to overwrite existing files in the destination backup directory. When this parameter is false, an exception is raised if an existing file is detected.
member.remarks: Precondition The database must be in backup state (that is, the online file backup operation must be bracketed by a beginBackup and commitBackup transaction pair).

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.BeginPartitionedFileBackup(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
The BeginPartitionedFileBackup method of the DatabaseFile class enables you to prepare to back up a subset of the partitions associated with a database file or to back up multiple partitions in parallel. Execution of this method backs up the partition control file and the partition index file. This method must be called before the first partition is backed up by using the BackupFilePartition method of the DatabaseFilePartition class.
If a parallel or selective backup of partitions is not required, use the backupFile method.
A beginPartitionedFileBackup method call must later be closed with an endPartitionedFileBackup method call.
An exception is raised if the database file is not partitioned.
member.param: Directory to back database file up to

Set the verifyChecksums parameter to true if you want checksums verified in the backed up file. Checksum verification is performed in a separate pass of the backed up file immediately after the copy phase. A checksum analysis of your backed up database files verifies that the files have not been corrupted by a hardware or environmental problem during the backup session. You should perform a separate checksum analysis of any backup that has been moved across media, especially if transferred across a network.
Set the compress parameter to true if you want to compress backed up data. You can compress data in a checked or an unchecked backup.

Set the overwriteDestination parameter to true if you want to allow file backups to overwrite existing files in the destination backup directory. When this parameter is false, an exception is raised if an existing file is detected.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.CertifyFile
member.summary: The CertifyFile method of the DatabaseFile class initiates an online certification of the physical database file; that is, it checks the database integrity. This operation can be initiated while the database is open for read and write access.
member.returns: The number of errors that were detected when certifying the database file

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.ChangeAccessMode(JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFileMode)
member.summary:
The ChangeAccessMode method of the DatabaseFile class changes the access mode of the file from read-only (DatabaseFileMode.ReadOnly) to update (DatabaseFileMode.Update), or the reverse. Use this method to allow applications to compact database files without shutting down the database. This method executes on a persistent server node, and is implemented and executed by the physical database engine.
When a file is changed to read-only mode, any threads that attempt to start a new transaction are first blocked, waiting for pending transactions to complete. All dirty buffers for objects resident in the file are flushed and the file is locked against further updates. At this point, threads blocked by the beginTransaction instruction are allowed to continue.
Any database operations that attempt to update a file in read-only mode receive an exception.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.CompactFile
member.summary: The CompactFile method of the DatabaseFile class initiates a compaction of the physical database file. This method returns the number of errors that were detected when compacting the database file. The CompactFile method executes on a persistent server node, and is implemented and executed by the physical database engine. A database file can be compacted while permitting concurrent transactions to update the file. Moreover, the compaction operation can be aborted without losing updates made by committed transactions.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.CreatePartition
member.summary:
The CreatePartition method of the DatabaseFile class creates a new empty database file partition and returns the partition identifier. You can make a number of related CreatePartition operations atomic, by containing them in the same database transaction.
When the CreatePartition operation is invoked on a partitioned database file, any transactions that attempt to create new instances are blocked until the transaction that invoked the CreatePartition operation commits or aborts.
An exception is raised if the database file is locked for reorganization or if the file is not partitioned.
member.returns: The partition identifier

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.EndPartitionedFileBackup
member.summary:
The EndPartitionedFileBackup method of the DatabaseFile class signals the end of a backup of selected database partitions. Call this method after the application has backed up the required partitions.
An exception is raised if the database file is not partitioned.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.Freeze
member.summary: The Freeze method of the DatabaseFile class converts a database file to read-only mode after which any object update, delete, or create operations are not be permitted. (See also the method.)
member.remarks: All objects in a frozen database file are automatically frozen, overriding individual volatility state.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.GetFileStatus
member.summary:
The GetFileStatus method of the DatabaseFile class returns the status of dropped physical database file during the backup session. The status of the database file is represented by DatabaseFileStatus enumeration.
You can use this method in backup applications to determine the status of database files prior to commencing the backup or to determine the status of database files returned in the droppedFiles parameter array passed to DatabaseAdmin class BackupAllDatabaseFiles and BackupDatabaseFiles methods.
This method executes on a persistent server node, and is implemented and executed by the physical database engine.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.GetFreeSpace(System.Decimal@)
member.summary:
The GetFreeSpace method of the DatabaseFile class evaluates the total amount of free space in the physical database file and returns the amount as a Decimal value.
You can initiate the free space evaluation operation while the database is open for read and write access. Restrictions for evaluating free space are those that apply when compacting a database file. (See also the method.)
This method executes on a persistent server node, and is implemented and executed by the physical database engine.
member.param: The number of errors encountered, if any, while performing the evaluation operation.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.GetBackupTimestamp
member.summary: The GetFullBackupTimestamp method of the DatabaseFile class returns a DateTime containing the date and time the full database file was stable in a backup where stable means the database file was not updated during the backup and therefore does not require recovery.
member.returns: The date and time the full database file was stable in a backup

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.GetCreationTimestamp
member.summary: The GetCreationTimestamp method of the DatabaseFile class returns a DateTime containing the date and time the database file was created.
member.returns: The date and time the database file was created

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.GetFileLength
member.summary: The GetFileLength method of the DatabaseFile class returns the size of the physical database file in bytes as a decimal value. This method executes on a persistent server node, and is implemented and executed by the physical database engine.
member.returns: The file size
member.remarks: The maximum size of a database file is 2^64-1 (or approximately 16 Exabytes), which requires 19 digits of precision for storage. To handle up to the maximum possible database file size, you should use a Decimal[19] primitive type to receive the return value.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.GetFullBackupTimestamp
member.summary: The GetFullBackupTimestamp method of the DatabaseFile class returns a DateTime containing the date and time the full database file was stable in a backup where stable means the database file was not updated during the backup and therefore does not require recovery.
member.returns: The date and time the full database file was stable in a backup

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.GetModifiedTimestamp
member.summary: The GetModifiedTimestamp method of the DatabaseFile class returns a DateTime containing the date and time the database file was last updated.
member.returns: The date and time the database file was last updated.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.GetName
member.summary: The GetName method of the DatabaseFile class returns the name of the database file, which is the file name without the .dat extension.
member.returns: The name of the database file without any extension

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.GetPartitionCount
member.summary:
The GetPartitionsCount method of the DatabaseFile class returns the number of non-removed partitions assigned to the database file.
An exception is raised if the database file is not partitioned.
member.returns: Number of non-removed partitions assigned to the database file

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.GetPartitionModulus
member.summary:
The GetPartitionModulus method of the DatabaseFile class returns the number of partitions of a database file in which new instances are stored.
An exception is raised if the database file is not partitioned.
member.returns: Number of partitions of a database file in which new instances are stored

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.GetStatistics
member.summary: The GetStatistics method of the DatabaseFile class returns statistics relating to read and write operations on the persistent database file represented by the DatabaseFile instance used as the method receiver.
member.returns: JoobDynamicObject object containing read and write operation statistics
member.remarks:
The values are returned as Integer64 properties in the returned dynamic object The properties returned in the JoobDynamicObject are listed in the following table.
Property Description
logicalReads The total number of read requests
logicalWrites The total number of write requests
logicalReadBytes The total accumulated size for all read requests
logicalWriteBytes The total accumulated size for all write requests
physicalReads The actual number of file read operations
physicalWrites The actual number of file write operations
physicalReadBytes The actual accumulated size for all file read operations
physicalWriteBytes The actual accumulated size for all file write operations
The logical counts record the number and size of requests that can be serviced in cache, whereas the physical counts record actual disk activity.
The returned values include cumulative counters, which are not reset during the lifetime of the database server node.
You need to compare values from one execution of the getStatistics method with the previous values, to work out the differences.
The cumulative values are held as 64-bit unsigned integers, which are copied to the dynamic object as Integer64 values. The maximum value before they wrap around to negative values is therefore 2^63 - 1 (approximately 8 Exabytes).

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.IsFrozen
member.summary: The IsFrozen method of the DatabaseFile class returns true if the associated database file has been frozen; otherwise it returns false. (See also the and methods.)
member.returns: Indicates if the DatabaseFile is frozen or not

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.IsOpen
member.summary: The IsOpen method of the DatabaseFile class returns true if the database file is currently open; otherwise it returns false.
member.returns: Indicates if the DatabaseFile is currently open or not

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.IsPartitioned
member.summary: The IsPartitioned method of the DatabaseFile class returns true if the associated database file is partitioned; otherwise it returns false. (See also the and methods.)
member.returns: Indicates if the DatabaseFile is partitioned or not

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.SetPartitionModulus(System.Int32)
member.summary:
The SetPartitionModulus method of the DatabaseFile class specifies the number of partitions in which new instances are stored through the value the modulus parameter, which is in the range 1 through 1,024. This sliding window is referred to as the creation window, as it defines the subset or window of partitions in which new objects are created. Expanding the creation window to include frozen, or offline, partitions is not allowed.
An exception is raised if the database file is locked for reorganization, the file is not partitioned, the specified partition modulus is a value outside the range of 1 through 1024, or the new modulus value would include frozen or offline partitions in the creation window.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.SetPartitioned(System.Boolean)
member.summary: The SetPartitioned method of the DatabaseFile class changes the partitioned attribute of an empty database file (that is, a non-instantiated database file),
member.param: Set file to be partitioned or not

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.Thaw
member.summary: The Thaw method of the DatabaseFile class restores the database file to its default active state. This brings the volatility of individual objects back into effect, allowing non-frozen objects to be updated or deleted. (See also the method.)

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.GetCryptStatus
member.summary: Returns the encryption status of the file
member.returns: Current encryption status of this file

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.GetDatabaseFileForClass``1
member.summary: The static getDbFile method of the DatabaseFile class returns the DatabaseFile instance for the database file in which instances of the specified class are located.
member.returns: The DatabaseFile instance for the database file in which instances of the specified class are located

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.MoveObjectToPartition(JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobObject,System.Int64)
member.summary: The static MoveObjectToPartition method of the DatabaseFile class moves an object to a specified partition within its enclosing database file.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile.SetObjectPartitionIndex(JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobObject,System.Int32)
member.summary: The static SetObjectPartitionIndex method of the DatabaseFile class specifies the partition in which to locate the receiver. It must be called within a transaction. Calling this method outside of the creating transaction has no effect.
member.param: The relative partition index, where 0 is the most recent partition, 1 the next most recent, etc.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DynamicExplicitInverseRef.AddDynamicInverse(JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.Property,JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.InverseUpdateMode,JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.InverseReferenceKind)
member.summary: This method adds an inverse between two dynamic properties. The property parameter must identify a compatible dynamic property. For example, if the type of the property is a Collection class the schema type of the receiver must compatible with the membership of the Collection.
member.param: The other property that makes up the inverse
How the inverse is to be maintained
member.returns: New that now describes the receiver after the inverse has been added
member.remarks: The type of a primitive property cannot be changed to a reference, and vice versa.
If instances of either class exist and the dynamic properties have non-null values the inverse relationship is not validated or populated when the inverse definition is added.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DynamicExplicitInverseRef.ChangeDynamicProperty(System.String,System.Type,System.Int32,System.Byte)
member.summary:
This method changes an existing dynamic property. Changes to any or all of the , , , and values are supported.
No reorganization is required when the type of a dynamic property is changed. If the actual value of a dynamic property differs to the type of the property definition the value is converted when the value is retrieved. Dynamic property values are always stored using the type of the property definition when the value is updated.
member.param: New length for dynamic property (required for Binary, String and StringUtf8)
New precision for dynamic property (required for Decimal)
member.remarks: The type of a primitive property cannot be changed to a reference, and vice versa.

If the declared length of a Binary, String or StringUtf8 dynamic property is changed and longer values have been assigned, the value is truncated to the defined length when the property is accessed. The new length will be enforced when a new value is assigned to the property.

member.summary: This method changes an existing exclusive dynamic property.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.UserCollClass.AddExternalKey(System.Type,System.Int32,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
The addExternalKey method of the UserCollClass class adds an external key specification to a soft collection. Use the keyType to specify the primitive type for the key. For string and binary keys, you must specify the keyLength parameter. This parameter is ignored for keys of other primitive types. Set the descending parameter to true if you want keys sorted in descending order and the caseInsensitive parameter to true if case-sensitivity is not required. If you require multiple keys, call the addExternalKey method to define each key in sequence. To signify that all keys have been defined, call the endKeys method.
The following preconditions apply when adding keys to a soft collection.
The collection is empty
The member type has been specified by using the SetMembership method
The dictionary contains external key definitions only
The total concatenated key size does not exceed the current key size limit (512 characters)
The appropriate system exception is raised if any of these preconditions are violated.
member.param: The primitive type for this key
For string and binary keys, you must specify the keyLength parameter. This parameter is ignored for keys of other primitive types
Set to true if you want keys sorted in descending order
Set to true if case-sensitivity is not required

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.UserCollClass.AddMemberKey(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
The addMemberKey method of the UserCollClass class adds a member key specification to a soft collection. If you require multiple keys, call the addMemberKey method to define each key in sequence. To signify that all keys have been defined, call the endKeys method. Specify a key path by passing a key-path expression in the propertyName parameter; for example, “shipment.supplier.name”. Set the descending parameter to true if you want keys sorted in descending order and the caseInsensitive parameter to true if case-sensitivity is not required.
The following preconditions apply when adding keys to a soft collection.
The collection is empty
The member type has been specified by using the SetMembership method
The dictionary contains member key definitions only
The parameter represents a valid property for the member type
The total concatenated key size does not exceed the current key size limit (512 characters)
The appropriate system exception is raised if any of these preconditions are violated.
member.param: Specify a key path by passing a key-path expression; for example, “shipment.supplier.name”.
Set to true if you want keys sorted in descending order
Set to true if case-sensitivity is not required

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.UserCollClass.ClearKeys
member.summary: The ClearKeys method of the UserCollClass class clears existing key definitions so that the dictionary can be reused.
member.remarks: Before the ClearKeys method is called, the collection must be empty; that is, it cannot contain data. If this precondition is violated, the appropriate system exception is raised.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.UserCollClass.EndKeys(System.Boolean)
member.summary: The EndKeys method of the UserCollClass class signifies the end of a single or multiple key specification. Use the parameter to specify whether the dictionary allows or disallows duplicate key entries. At least one key must have been defined (by using the or method). If this precondition is violated, the appropriate system exception is raised.
member.param: Set to true to specify whether the dictionary allows or disallows duplicate key entries.
member.remarks: At least one key must have been defined (by using the or method). If this precondition is violated, the appropriate system exception is raised.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.UserCollClass.SetLength(System.Int32,System.Byte)
member.summary: The SetMembership method of the UserCollClass class sets the membership (that is, the base type for members) of the soft collection. Before the SetMembership method is called, the collection must be empty; that is, it cannot contain data. If this precondition is violated, the appropriate system exception is raised. This method implicitly calls the method.
member.remarks: Note soft collections can have object members only; that is, these dictionaries cannot have primitive type membership.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.UserCollClass.IsValid
member.summary: The IsValid method of the UserCollClass class checks to see if the dictionary is fully defined; that is after the method is called.
member.returns: true when the soft collection is fully defined, otherwise false

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.UserCollClass.SetMembership(System.String)
member.summary: The SetMembership method of the UserCollClass class sets the membership (that is, the base type for members) of the soft collection. Before the SetMembership method is called, the collection must be empty; that is, it cannot contain data. If this precondition is violated, the appropriate system exception is raised. This method implicitly calls the method.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobCollection`1.GetEnumerator
member.summary: Gets the enumerator. The enumerator will share lock the collection facilitating a consistent view of the collection. The collection will be unlocked in the following scenarios: 1) The enumeration is terminated (in the case of a foreach) 2) The enumerator is disposed or finalized.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobCollection`1.GetSharedProxyEnumerator
member.summary: Gets the enumerator. The enumerator will share lock the collection facilitating a consistent view of the collection. The collection will be unlocked in the following scenarios: 1) The enumeration is terminated (in the case of a foreach) 2) The enumerator is disposed or finalized.
member.remarks:
This enumerator will RE-USE the same .NET object. This means that when the position of the enumerator changes the data associated with the .NET object returned will change but any references to the .NET object will be UNCHANGED. This enumerator is therefore only appropriate when obtaining data from the object and NO REFERENCES will be held to the .NET object outside the scope of the loop.
If a reference to the .NET object is required outside the enumerator loop, then create a new .Net proxy object using code similar to the following can be used JoobObject obj = JoobContext.CurrentContext.FindInstance<JoobObject>(((JoobObject)iter.Current).ObjectId);

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.DynamicDictionary`1.AddExternalKey(System.Type,System.Int32,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary:
The addExternalKey method of the DynaDictionary class adds an external key specification to a dynamic dictionary. Use the keyType to specify the primitive type for the key. For string and binary keys, you must specify the keyLength parameter. This parameter is ignored for keys of other primitive types. Set the descending parameter to true if you want keys sorted in descending order and the caseInsensitive parameter to true if case-sensitivity is not required. If you require multiple keys, call the addExternalKey method to define each key in sequence. To signify that all keys have been defined, call the endKeys method.
The following preconditions apply when adding keys to a dynamic dictionary.
The collection is empty
The member type has been specified by using the SetMembership method
The dictionary contains external key definitions only
The total concatenated key size does not exceed the current key size limit (512 characters)
The appropriate system exception is raised if any of these preconditions are violated.
member.param: The primitive type for this key
For string and binary keys, you must specify the keyLength parameter. This parameter is ignored for keys of other primitive types
Set to true if you want keys sorted in descending order
Set to true if case-sensitivity is not required

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.DynamicDictionary`1.AddMemberKey(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary:
The addMemberKey method of the DynaDictionary class adds a member key specification to a dynamic dictionary. If you require multiple keys, call the addMemberKey method to define each key in sequence. To signify that all keys have been defined, call the endKeys method. Specify a key path by passing a key-path expression in the propertyName parameter; for example, “shipment.supplier.name”. Set the descending parameter to true if you want keys sorted in descending order and the caseInsensitive parameter to true if case-sensitivity is not required.
The following preconditions apply when adding keys to a dynamic dictionary.
The collection is empty
The member type has been specified by using the SetMembership method
The dictionary contains member key definitions only
The parameter represents a valid property for the member type
The total concatenated key size does not exceed the current key size limit (512 characters)
The appropriate system exception is raised if any of these preconditions are violated.
member.param: Specify a key path by passing a key-path expression; for example, “shipment.supplier.name”.
Set to true if you want keys sorted in descending order
Set to true if case-sensitivity is not required

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.DynamicDictionary`1.EndKeys(System.Boolean)
member.summary: The EndKeys method of the DynamicDictionary class signifies the end of a single or multiple key specification. Use the parameter to specify whether the dictionary allows or disallows duplicate key entries. At least one key must have been defined (by using the or method). If this precondition is violated, the appropriate system exception is raised.
member.param: Set to true to specify whether the dictionary allows or disallows duplicate key entries.
member.remarks: At least one key must have been defined (by using the or method). If this precondition is violated, the appropriate system exception is raised.

member.summary: The ClearKeys method of the DynamicDictionary class clears existing key definitions so that the dictionary can be reused.
member.remarks: Before the ClearKeys method is called, the collection must be empty; that is, it cannot contain data. If this precondition is violated, the appropriate system exception is raised.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.DynamicDictionary`1.DecodeKeyDescriptionV0(System.Collections.Generic.IList{System.String},System.String)
member.summary: Decode string containing description of dynamic dictionary
member.param: Description as saved in the database
member.remarks: Format in dynamic dictionary description membershipClassOid ";" "M" or "E" ";" // M == MemberKey, E == ExternalKey if "M" "+" propertyName "," descending , caseInsensitive ";" // descending "0" == ascending, other == descending; caseInsensitive "0" == sensitive, other == Insensitive if "E" "+" primTypeObjectId "," descending , caseInsensitive ";" // descending "0" == ascending, other == descending; caseInsensitive "0" == sensitive, other == Insensitive

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.DynamicDictionary`1.DecodeKeyDescriptionV1(System.Collections.Generic.IList{System.String},System.String)
member.summary: Decode string containing description of dynamic dictionary
member.param: Description as saved in the database
member.remarks: Format in dynamic dictionary description "V" version "#" classNumber ";" { "M" | "E" } ";" if "M" (MemberKey) "+" propertyName or keyPath "," {"C"|"I"|"P"} DskParamFormat "," length "," {"0"|"1"} (descending) "," {"0"|"1"} (caseInsensitive) "," sortOrder "," precision "," scaleFactor ";" if "E" (ExternalKey) "+" "," {"C" ClassNumber|"I" InterfaceNumber|"P" PrimitiveTypeNumber} "," length "," {"0"|"1"} (descending) "," {"0"|"1"} (caseInsensitive) "," sortOrder "," precision "," scaleFactor ";" "*" {"0"|"1"} (duplicatesAllowed)

member.name: T:JadeSoftware.Joob.ExtKeyDictionary`2
member.summary:
The ExternalKeyDictionary class encapsulates the behaviour required to access entries in external key dictionary subclasses.
External key dictionaries are dictionaries in which the keys are not derived from the properties in the member objects but are external values supplied as parameters to the access methods.

member.summary: The JoobBytes class has a membership of the Byte primitive type. It provides an efficient way to store and retrieve instances of unstructured data (such as text, graphic images, sound or video streams) of arbitrary size.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobBytes.Allocate(System.Int64)
member.summary: Allocates virtual storage on the receiver to hold binary content up to the specified length.
member.remarks: Use the Allocate method to build binary content in a piece-wise manner using the index properties. When data is loaded sequentially by using the AppendData method or in its entirety by using the LoadFromFile method, there is no need to allocate storage in advance. Use the Allocate method only on an empty JoobBytes object. If the JoobBytes object has a defined length greater than 0, use the Grow method to increase the length (the amount of virtual storage).

If the specified length is less than or equal to the length that has already been allocated, the method has no effect.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobBytes.ReadOnly
member.summary: The ReadOnly property of the JoobBytes class specifies whether the instance can be modified This property can be set to true only if the value of the DedicatedFile property is true When the value of the ReadOnly property is set to true, the instance is marked as read-only and attempts to update its content will raise an exception. In addition, the dedicated file has its Read-only attribute set to true, as a hint to external programs that the file contents should not be modified

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobGeometry.Buffer(System.Double,System.Int32)
member.summary: Calculates the geometry that contains, approximately, all points within a given of the current instance.
member.param: The buffer distance. A positive distance has an effect of dilation; a negative distance, erosion.
Specifies the number of segments used to approximate a quarter circle around a point
member.returns: The buffer geoemtry

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobGeometry.BufferWithStyle(System.Double,System.Int32,JadeSoftware.Joob.GeometryBufferCapStyle,JadeSoftware.Joob.GeometryBufferJoinStyle,System.Double)
member.summary: Calculates the geometry that contains, approximately, all points within a given of the current instance, with a given style.
member.param: The buffer distance. A positive distance has an effect of dilation; a negative distance, erosion.
The number of segments used to approximate a quarter circle around a point.
The limit on the mitre ratio used for very sharp corners
member.returns: The buffer geoemtry

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobGeometry.SingleSidedBuffer(System.Double,System.Int32,JadeSoftware.Joob.GeometryBufferJoinStyle,System.Double,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Calculates the geometry that contains, approximately, all points on one side within a given of the current instance, with a given style.
member.param: The buffer distance. A positive distance has an effect of dilation; a negative distance, erosion.
The number of segments used to approximate a quarter circle around a point.
The limit on the mitre ratio used for very sharp corners
Indicates the resulting buffer is to be on the left side
member.returns: The buffer geoemtry
member.remarks: This method is only supported for LINESTRING geometries.
